channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Epic raising the price of Fortnite V-Bucks because $5.7 billion isn’t enough to cover their costs, apparently.
item.content:encoded:
“The cost of running Fortnite has gone up a lot and we’re raising prices to help pay the bills,”  is the first and rather blunt line in a blog post from Epic Games.  This is the same Epic Games that made an estimated $5.7 billion in 2024.

As of March 19th the following changes are being made

V-Bucks Pack Tiers

Pack*

Previous

New

With Epic Rewards* 

$8.99 Pack

1,000 V-Bucks

800 V-Bucks

+$1.79 back

$22.99 Pack

2,800 V-Bucks

2,400 V-Bucks

+$4.59 back

$36.99 Pack

5,000 V-Bucks

4,500 V-Bucks

+$7.39 back

$89.99 Pack

13,500 V-Bucks

12,500 V-Bucks

+$17.99 back

Exact Amount Pack

~$0.50* for 50 V-Bucks

$0.99* for 50 V-Bucks

The Battle Pass has been reduced to 800 V-Bucks and you can earn 800 V-Bucks if you complete it, but the 500 Bonus Awards has been scrapped.  The cost of the Music and LEGO pass have also been reduced to 1,200 V-Bucks, Fortnite Crew will change from 1,000 to 800 V-Bucks, and the Battle Bundle (Battle Pass + 25 Levels) will lower from 2,800 to 2,600 V-Bucks.

Source: Epic Games



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/10/epic-raising-fortnite-v-bucks-because-5-7-billion-isnt-enough-to-cover-their-costs-apparently/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Invincible VS open beta taking place in April for PS5 and Xbox Series XS
item.content:encoded:
Skybound has announced that its 3v3 fighter Invincible VS, based on the show and comic Invincible, will be getting an open beta on PS5 and Xbox Series XS. There are a few weeks to wait before you will be able to try Invincible VS out, with the open beta set to take place from April 9th to April 11th. The news was announced through the new Allen The Alien gameplay trailer.

The Invincible VS open beta is pretty stacked giving players access to ten of the fighters and six arenas. The fighters include Invincible, Omni-Man, Atom Eve, Rex Splode, Monster Girl, Bulletproof, Robot, Thula, Battle Beast, and Allen The Alien. The arenas that players will be able to battle in are Hometown Invasion (Night), The Moon, Ka-Hors Tomb (Day), Himaylayas, Kinetic Chamber, and the Training Room. The full roster of Invincible will include 18 fighters.

Invincible VS is due to release on April 30th for PS5, Xbox Series XS, and PC.

Source: Invincible VS official site



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/10/invincible-vs-open-beta-taking-place-in-april-for-ps5-and-xbox-series-xs/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fallout 3 Remastered named on collectables site
item.content:encoded:
McFarlane Toys, creators of super fancy and rather pricey premium collectables, have seemingly confirmed a remaster of Fallout 3.

A listing on BroDepot for a Nuka Cola bottle is listed as “CFARLANE ELITE EDITION 7IN – FALLOUT 3 REMASTERED – #13 T-45B NUKA COLA”

The product has a release date of 31st August 2026 and one would assume that the collectable would be released close to the time of the Remaster.

Bethesda have not announced Fallout 3 Remastered, but there have been many rumours and leaks that it is being developed. Most notably, it was listed in the  FTC v. Microsoft, lawsuit, although back then, it had a release date of 2024 which clearly never happened. The recent remaster of Oblivion sold very well. Fallout 4 can still be played on current-gen consoles, so Fallout 3 seems the obvious choice to remaster next.

The success of the Fallout TV show on Amazon has also helped bring the franchise back to the fore. Season 2 ended at the start of February, and Amazon is set to start filming season 3 at the start of May, with an expected release sometime near the end of 2027.

Fun Fact: One of my first jobs at TSA was to review every piece of Fallout 3 DLC, one after another.

Our original Fallout 3 review from 2008 is still online. “What is slightly worrying is the buggy nature of the game,” said our reviewer, perhaps understating things a little.  “Don’t let this put you off too much, though, Fallout 3 is a wonderful game for fans of the genre.  It’s humourous when it wants to be, yet also deep and worryingly tragic when required to be.  For fans of the previous games wary of what Bethesda would do to their beloved series this is a godsend even if it’s not really a true sequel in every sense of the word, and for anyone wanting something to last them through the Winter this is a must-have. ”

Source: BroDepot



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/10/fallout-3-remastered-named-on-collectables-site/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Beyond These Stars is now skipping early access
item.content:encoded:
Back in November it was reported that Beyond These Stars early access was delayed to 2026, but today there has been a further update regarding an early access release. Short version, it isn’t happening. Instead, Balancing Monkey Games is going to skip early access and head straight for the 1.0 release of Beyond These Stars later this year, though a date is yet to be confirmed.

In a blog post the development team wrote, “While preparing for what was planned to be an Early Access release, we found that many of our systems were complete enough to meet our requirements for a 1.0 release. After LOTS of discussions in-house, we’ve decided that with a final, strong push on content and gameplay, we’ll be able to have a completed version of the game in 2026. We know you all have been waiting patiently to enter the world of Beyond These Stars, and we’re so excited that you’ll be able to experience it in a fully realized form, rather than just a chapter of it, with a 1.0 release.”

Balancing Monkey Games also confirmed what additional content players will see in version 1.0, which would not have been in an early access build of Beyond These Stars. The content includes the full galaxy to explore, new complex buildings, interaction mechanics for the peeps, more space to build your civilisation, planets that can be settled, alien species to meet, quests to undertake, and the full story mode of the game. In Beyond These Stars,  the Peeps find themselves living on the back of the space whale Kewa, which gives them an opportunity to build a home. However, there will be challenges for players to face when building a society on top of a sentient creature including your relationship with it. Expand too quickly and overwork the land on Kewa, and there will be fewer resources with the land shrinking.

Source: Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/10/beyond-these-stars-is-now-skipping-early-access/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem is getting story DLC and more
item.content:encoded:
Koshi Nakanishi,  the director of Resident Evil Requiem, has revealed that a Story Expansion for the game is currently in development.

“In this story we will delve deeper into the world of Requiem,” he said in an announcement on Twitter. “We’re hard at work at it now. It will take some time, so we ask for your patience and hope you will look forward to it.”

He also announced that the game will be getting a photo mode and sometime in May, they will be adding an unspecified minigame. No other details about the Story DLC were revealed so we don’t know if you will be playing as Leon, Grace, or another character.

Resident Evil Requiem launched a few weeks ago and gained many favourable reviews, including a 9/10 from us.

“Resident Evil Requiem is a superb entry in the series and feels like a ‘best of’ at times with the persistent Easter eggs, references, and gameplay callbacks to the full range of earlier games,” said Steve, “There is a vast amount of wider lore for fans of that part of Capcom’s legendary titles, but even newcomers will be caught up in the narrative and balls to the wall gameplay. RE Requiem is up there with the very best of the series.”

You can also read our thoughts in the very impressive Switch 2 version of the game. 

Source: Twitter



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/10/resident-evil-requiem-is-getting-story-dlc-and-more/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
